 {"id":"frankenredis-1ng7","title":"Implement command propagation from primary to connected replicas","description":"# Command Propagation to Replicas

## What
When FrankenRedis is acting as a primary and replicas are connected, write commands must be propagated to all connected replicas in real-time.

## Current State
The fr-server has `replication_follow_up_bytes()` which serializes commands for replication. Replica connections are tracked. The gap is that write commands are not actually pushed to replica write buffers after execution, and the backlog buffer is not populated.

## Implementation
1. After each write command execution in the main event loop, check if there are connected replicas
2. Serialize the command as RESP (already done by replication_follow_up_bytes)
3. Push the serialized bytes into each replica connection write_buf AND the backlog buffer
4. Advance replication offset by the number of bytes pushed
5. Handle slow replicas: if write_buf exceeds output_buffer_limit, disconnect replica with warning
6. Ensure read-only commands (GET, KEYS, etc.) are NOT propagated

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: Write command increments replication offset
- Test 2: Read command does NOT increment replication offset
- Test 3: Offset advances by exact RESP-serialized byte count
- Test 4: MULTI/EXEC propagates all commands in the transaction

## Integration Tests
- Test 5: Start primary, connect replica, SET 1000 keys on primary, verify all 1000 appear on replica
- Test 6: Slow replica (inject delay) — verify primary disconnects it after buffer overflow
- Test 7: Multiple replicas — verify all receive the same commands
- Test 8: INFO replication on primary shows connected_slaves count

## Logging
- Log propagation: "REPL: propagating SET key (42 bytes) to 2 replicas"
- Log offset advancement
- Log slow replica disconnection with reason

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need a connection to propagate to
- Backlog buffer (iz1j) — propagated bytes go here for partial resync
- RDB snapshot streaming (de0t) — replica must have initial state before streaming

## Files
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (propagation after command dispatch)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (offset tracking)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.983808258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:38.898911059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:38.898680643Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: propagate_writes_to_replicas() at main.rs:2255 pushes AOF-serialized writes to all connected replica write buffers. Called in main event loop at line 534 after every command batch. Unit test at line 3564 verifies the flow.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:44.677262603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-de0t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:14:57.688577941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.790873067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1qdg","title":"Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands","description":"# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-ajk","title":"Implement real XINFO CONSUMERS pending count and idle/inactive time","description":"## Problem

fr-store/src/lib.rs line 5796: xinfo_consumers returns Vec<Vec<u8>> (just consumer names).
fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4169-4179: stream_consumer_info_to_frame hardcodes pending=0, idle=0, inactive=0.

## What Redis Returns

XINFO CONSUMERS key group returns per-consumer:
- name: consumer name
- pending: number of PEL entries owned by this consumer
- idle: milliseconds since last interaction (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XACK)
- inactive: milliseconds since last successful XREADGROUP (Redis 7.2+)

## Implementation Plan

1. Change Store::xinfo_consumers to return Vec<(Vec<u8>, usize, u64, u64)> — (name, pending_count, last_interaction_ms, last_read_ms)
2. Compute pending_count by scanning the PEL and counting entries where consumer matches
3. Track last_interaction_ms per consumer in StreamGroupState (update on XREADGROUP, XCLAIM, XACK)
4. Track last_read_ms per consumer (update only on XREADGROUP with >)
5. Update stream_consumer_info_to_frame to use real values

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: StreamGroupState consumer tracking, xinfo_consumers
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: stream_consumer_info_to_frame","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:21.561101730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:44.406972571Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:44.406145205Z","close_reason":"XINFO CONSUMERS now returns real pending count and idle time computed from PEL, not hardcoded zeros","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-ajk","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7o5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:31.307893656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-as1","title":"Wire CONFIG SET maxclients to fr-server connection acceptance","description":"## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 33 defines `const MAX_CLIENTS: usize = 10_000` and uses it at line 499 to reject new connections. CONFIG SET maxclients updates the config table in fr-runtime but never propagates to fr-server's actual enforcement.

## What Redis Does

Redis's CONFIG SET maxclients immediately changes the rlimit and connection acceptance threshold. New connections beyond the limit get an error response and are closed.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add a `max_clients` field to `ServerState` in fr-runtime (initialized to 10000)
2. In `handle_config_set`, when maxclients is set, update `self.server.max_clients`
3. In fr-server's main loop, read `runtime.server.max_clients` (or expose via a getter) instead of the const
4. Also update `store.server_maxclients` so INFO reports the correct value
5. Remove the `MAX_CLIENTS` const from fr-server

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Add field to ServerState, handle in config_set
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace const with runtime read
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`: Already has server_maxclients field (added in f8c5a8a)

## Testing

- Unit test: CONFIG SET maxclients 100 → CONFIG GET maxclients returns 100
- Unit test: INFO clients section reports the new maxclients value
- Integration: verify connection rejection beyond the configured limit (may need fr-conformance fixture)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:25.451789990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:12.846797806Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:12.845683417Z","close_reason":"maxclients wired: ServerState.max_clients + CONFIG SET/GET + fr-server reads runtime value","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-as1","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:28.584373097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-b02","title":"Fix Lua string.format missing argument and pattern capture sentinel","description":"## Problem 1: string.format silent nil substitution

fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs line 3850: When string.format has more format specifiers than arguments, args.get(arg_idx) returns None which becomes LuaValue::Nil. Real Lua 5.1 raises \"bad argument #N to 'format' (string expected, got no value)\".

Fix: Check if arg_idx >= args.len() before accessing, and return an error.

## Problem 2: LuaCapture::Substring(start, 0) sentinel ambiguity

fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs line 3527: LuaCapture::Substring(start, 0) is used as a sentinel for \"unclosed capture\" (end not yet determined). But 0 is a legitimate end position for an empty capture at the start of the string (e.g., pattern \"()\" at position 0). This causes incorrect pattern matching behavior for these edge cases.

Fix: Use a dedicated sentinel value. Options:
- Use Option<usize> for the end: Substring(usize, Option<usize>) where None = unclosed
- Use usize::MAX as sentinel (captures can't be that long)
- Add a separate variant: OpenCapture(usize) vs Substring(usize, usize)

Recommendation: Option<usize> is cleanest and most Rust-idiomatic.

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs: Both fixes in the same file

## Testing

- string.format(\"%s%s\", \"a\") should error, not return \"anil\"
- Pattern \"()\" at start of string should capture position 1 (Lua 1-indexed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:53.496167480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:47:37.201427655Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:47:37.200430598Z","close_reason":"string.format errors on missing args instead of silent nil; LuaCapture::Substring uses Option<usize> for end position","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-b02","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7vn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:57.253625606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-icsm","title":"EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking","description":"# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic. The benchmark harness must exist before any optimization work can be proven.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:22.979891472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057699153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057479858Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: benchmark harness (bxlo), baselines (gess), regression gate (1zxx) all closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1zxx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.512824074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-bxlo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.348066219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gess","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.429198141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-irk8","title":"EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in","description":"# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.896335079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.672075914Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.671896515Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-06pn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.436452385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-42df","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.828280032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-6axy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.963579323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-e1e9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.699590804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-v7zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.569119622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-rk0","title":"Align replication offsets with byte-stream semantics","description":"Replication currently advances primary/ack offsets by one per AOF record, while PSYNC backlog replay and replica propagation operate on RESP byte streams. This misstates ROLE/INFO replication offsets and risks incorrect partial-resync boundaries once command sizes vary. Update offset accounting and backlog slicing to use encoded stream byte offsets, then add tests covering ROLE/INFO and partial-resync boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:45:03.118565425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:51:57.059822715Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T23:51:57.059239256Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
